steps.title: Isolate the period
steps.content: The equation is $s=\frac{5}{3}p$. To solve for $p$, multiply both sides by the reciprocal of $\frac{5}{3}$.

steps.title: Use the reciprocal
steps.content: Multiply by $\frac{3}{5}$ and simplify. $$ \frac{3}{5}s=\frac{3}{5}\cdot\frac{5}{3}p $$ $$ \frac{3}{5}s=p $$ $$ p=\frac{3}{5}s $$ The answer is A.
